content:
💡 Shadowbroker | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a unified, real-time geospatial intelligence platform for monitoring global activities across various domains, including aviation, maritime, space, and geopolitical events.
✨ Key Features: • Real-time multi-domain OSINT dashboard • Unified dark-ops map interface • Tracks aircraft, ships, satellites, earthquakes, conflict zones, CCTV networks, GPS jamming, and geopolitical events
📖 Summary: ShadowBroker is a real-time, multi-domain OSINT dashboard that aggregates live data from dozens of open-source intelligence feeds and displays them on a unified map interface. It tracks aircraft, ships, satellites, earthquakes, conflict zones, CCTV networks, and geopolitical events, updating all information in real time.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 Cosmos-Server | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Securing and simplifying the management of self-hosted applications on a home server.
✨ Key Features: • App Store for easy installation and management • Storage Manager with Parity Disks and MergerFS • Network Storages based on RClone • Reverse-Proxy with automatic HTTPS • Authentication Server with MFA and multiple strategies
📖 Summary: Cosmos is a self-hosted home server solution designed to enhance the security and ease of managing personal applications. It acts as a secure gateway and server manager, offering features like an app store, storage management, reverse proxy, and authentication server to protect self-hosted services.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 hexhog | Rust
🎯 Primary Use Case: Editing and inspecting the raw byte content of files.
✨ Key Features: • Configurable hex viewer/editor • Basic hex editing (edit/delete/insert bytes) • Select and copy/paste bytes • Installation via cargo, AUR, and Homebrew • Customizable themes and character sets
📖 Summary: Hexhog is a configurable hex viewer and editor written in Rust. It allows users to inspect and modify the byte-level content of files directly, offering basic editing features like byte insertion, deletion, copying, and pasting.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 pluGET | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating the process of updating plugins and server software for Minecraft servers to save time and ensure all components are up-to-date.
✨ Key Features: • Automated plugin and server software updates • Local and remote server management (SFTP/FTP) • Support for Spigot, Modrinth, and GitHub plugin sources • Updates for PaperMC, Purpur, Waterfall, and Velocity server software • Console-based operation with command line arguments
📖 Summary: pluGET is a Python-based package manager designed to automate the updating of plugins and server software for Minecraft servers. It supports local and remote server management via SFTP/FTP, integrating with APIs from Spigot, Modrinth, and GitHub to simplify plugin and server software maintenance.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 librepods | Kotlin
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling advanced AirPods features on non-Apple devices.
✨ Key Features: • Noise Control Modes • Ear Detection • Battery Status • Head Gestures • Conversational Awareness
📖 Summary: LibrePods aims to liberate Apple AirPods features from the Apple ecosystem, enabling users to access premium functionalities like noise control, ear detection, and battery status on non-Apple devices, primarily Android and Linux.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
